title: Build issue with abc submodule
body:
### Version

Happening on the most recent commit, as well as commits from May and March

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

Build Yosys on Ubuntu 20.04 with gcc (Ubuntu 9.4.0-1ubuntu1~20.04.2) 9.4.0.

### Expected Behavior

Yosys builds successfully.

### Actual Behavior

I see the following errors:

```
/usr/bin/ld: src/sat/glucose/Options.o: relocation R_X86_64_32 against symbol `_ZGVZN3abc5Gluco6Option13getOptionListEvE7options' can not be used when making a PIE object; recompile with -fPIE
/usr/bin/ld: src/sat/glucose/SimpSolver.o: relocation R_X86_64_32S against symbol `_ZTVN3abc5Gluco10SimpSolverE' can not be used when making a PIE object; recompile with -fPIE
/usr/bin/ld: src/sat/glucose/System.o: relocation R_X86_64_32 against `.rodata.str1.1' can not be used when making a PIE object; recompile with -fPIE
/usr/bin/ld: src/sat/glucose2/Glucose2.o: relocation R_X86_64_32S against symbol `_ZTVN3abc6Gluco26SolverE' can not be used when making a PIE object; recompile with -fPIE
...
```

This feels like it must be on my side, because I've never seen this before and it seems to be happening on every commit I try!

/usr/bin/ld: src/sat/glucose/SimpSolver.o: relocation R_X86_64_32S against symbol `_ZTVN3abc5Gluco10SimpSolverE' can not be used when making a PIE object; recompile with -fPIE
